# Project Fundamentals and Design Context -- CCDash

> **Companion artifacts:** project-purpose-and-feature-catalog (feature inventory), research-agent-context-pack (researcher briefing), project-opportunity-map (forward-looking ideas)

> **How to read this document.** Captures the project's design DNA: architectural patterns, decisions, constraints, and what future contributors must understand first. Every claim is tagged `[evidence]`, `[inference]`, or `[open]` with confidence (`H/M/L`) and cited.

## Architectural Overview

CCDash is a layered, profile-driven FastAPI + React application whose central organizing principle is **filesystem-as-source-of-truth**: the cache database (SQLite by default, Postgres by opt-in) is a *derived* index computed by a continuously-running sync engine that walks the project tree, parses session logs and markdown, and writes normalized rows into repositories `[evidence H]` (`backend/db/sync_engine.py`, `backend/db/file_watcher.py`, `backend/parsers/`, `backend/db/repositories/`). Every domain object -- session, document, feature, task, test run, execution -- has a parser, a repository, and edges in a universal link graph `[evidence H]` (`backend/db/repositories/entity_graph.py`, `backend/document_linking.py`).

The backend enforces a strict **Router - Service - Repository** layering convention. Routers in `backend/routers/` never touch raw SQL; they call services in `backend/services/` which in turn call repositories in `backend/db/repositories/` `[evidence H]` (`CLAUDE.md`, `backend/routers/`, `backend/services/`, `backend/db/repositories/`). The same backend runs under four distinct runtime profiles -- `local`, `api`, `worker`, `test` -- each with its own capability set (watch, sync, jobs, auth, integrations) and its own bootstrap module `[evidence H]` (`backend/runtime/profiles.py`, `backend/runtime/bootstrap_local.py`, `backend/runtime/bootstrap_api.py`, `backend/runtime/bootstrap_worker.py`, `backend/runtime/bootstrap_test.py`, `backend/runtime/container.py`).

The frontend is a React 19 SPA served by Vite and rendered into `index.tsx`, with all page routes wired in `App.tsx` under a HashRouter `[evidence H]` (`App.tsx`, `index.tsx`, `vite.config.ts`). State is organized as a **split shell** of React Contexts: AppSessionContext (project/session selection), AppEntityDataContext (sessions/documents/tasks/features data), AppRuntimeContext (polling, loading, runtime health), with DataContext acting as a compatibility facade for the useData() hook used across components `[evidence H]` (`contexts/DataContext.tsx`, `contexts/AppSessionContext.tsx`, `contexts/AppEntityDataContext.tsx`, `contexts/AppRuntimeContext.tsx`, `CLAUDE.md`).

Observability is first-class: `backend/observability/otel.py` wires OpenTelemetry spans and metrics for ingestion, parser failures, tool calls, tokens, cost, and telemetry-export lifecycle, with a fallback Prometheus client if OTEL is disabled `[evidence H]` (`backend/observability/otel.py`, `backend/config.py`). Background work (sync, telemetry export, analytics snapshots) runs through a job adapter layer at `backend/adapters/jobs/` coordinated by the worker bootstrap `[evidence H]` (`backend/adapters/jobs/runtime.py`, `backend/adapters/jobs/telemetry_exporter.py`, `backend/worker.py`).

## Important Design Decisions

Top 5 load-bearing decisions ranked by recency plus cross-codebase footprint. Remaining decisions listed in the supplemental table below.

| Decision | Rationale (cited) | Tradeoffs accepted | Source | Tag | Footprint |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Filesystem is source of truth; cache DB is rebuildable index | Fits local-first workflow; lets users freely edit session/plan files and rely on sync to reflect reality | Accepts sync cost and hash-tracking complexity in `backend/db/sync_engine.py` | `backend/db/sync_engine.py`, `backend/db/file_watcher.py` | `[evidence H]` | global |
| Profile-based runtime (local/api/worker/test) with distinct bootstraps | Lets the same codebase run as a single-process desktop app, a split API/worker pair, or a test harness without branching | Forces every feature to consider profile capabilities (watch, sync, jobs, auth, integrations) | `backend/runtime/profiles.py`, `backend/runtime/bootstrap.py`, `backend/runtime/container.py` | `[evidence H]` | global |
| Canonical session transcript storage (session_messages) | Enables semantic search, derived intelligence facts, and multi-platform parsing without re-reading JSONL | Adds a projection layer and migration burden; historical backfill required for enterprise | `backend/services/session_transcript_contract.py`, `backend/services/session_transcript_projection.py`, `backend/db/repositories/session_messages.py`, `CHANGELOG.md` | `[evidence H]` | global |
| Gated, policy-first local execution runtime | Lets the workbench run local commands safely with approval gates and sandboxed subprocesses | Adds UI + service complexity to every execution path | `backend/services/execution_policy.py`, `backend/services/execution_runtime.py`, `backend/routers/execution.py` | `[evidence H]` | local |
| Opt-in telemetry export with hardened queue + retention | Keeps the product local-first by default while allowing enterprise upstream export | Requires a dedicated queue table, job runner, and observability instrumentation | `backend/services/integrations/telemetry_exporter.py`, `backend/adapters/jobs/telemetry_exporter.py`, `backend/db/repositories/telemetry_queue.py`, `CHANGELOG.md` | `[evidence H]` | local |

**Supplemental decisions** (ADRs + inferred):

| Decision | Source | Tag |
|---|---|---|
| VSCode extension should use SSE rather than polling | `docs/project_plans/adrs/adr-001-vscode-extension-realtime-sse-over-polling.md` | `[evidence M]` |
| MCP server should be a separate process from the VSCode extension | `docs/project_plans/adrs/adr-002-mcp-server-separate-from-extension.md` | `[evidence H]` |
| Agent query layer should be transport-neutral (CLI/MCP/extension share it) | `docs/project_plans/adrs/adr-003-transport-neutral-agent-query-layer.md` | `[evidence H]` |
| MCP server uses dual-transport strategy | `docs/project_plans/adrs/adr-004-mcp-server-dual-transport-strategy.md` | `[evidence H]` |
| CLI framework is Typer | `docs/project_plans/adrs/adr-005-cli-framework-selection-typer.md` | `[evidence H]` |
| SQLite by default, Postgres via opt-in env flag | `backend/db/connection.py`, `backend/config.py` | `[evidence H]` |
| Split-shell React provider architecture over a single monolithic context | `contexts/AppSessionContext.tsx`, `contexts/AppEntityDataContext.tsx`, `contexts/AppRuntimeContext.tsx`, `CLAUDE.md` | `[evidence H]` |

## Implicit Design Philosophy

- **Local-first is a value, not a limitation.** The defaults are SQLite, filesystem watchers, single-process, single-user. Enterprise features are explicit opt-ins (CCDASH_STORAGE_SHARED_POSTGRES, CCDASH_TELEMETRY_EXPORT_ENABLED, CCDASH_ENTERPRISE_FILESYSTEM_INGESTION_ENABLED) rather than the norm `[inference H]` (`backend/config.py`, `backend/runtime/profiles.py`).
- **Plan-driven development over TODO comments.** The repo has effectively zero inline TODO/FIXME/XXX/HACK tokens in backend/, but rich `.claude/progress/` + `docs/project_plans/implementation_plans/` trees `[inference H]`. Work is tracked as plan + progress files, not scattered comments.
- **Evidence-linked entities over free-floating records.** Nearly every domain object carries a link edge or a source reference. The entity_links table is universal, not feature-specific `[evidence H]` (`backend/db/repositories/entity_graph.py`, `backend/document_linking.py`).
- **Feature flags that default off are a release strategy, not experimental debt.** Flags like CCDASH_TEST_VISUALIZER_ENABLED, CCDASH_INTEGRITY_SIGNALS_ENABLED, CCDASH_LIVE_TEST_UPDATES_ENABLED default off and ship with UI routes present; flags like CCDASH_SESSION_USAGE_ATTRIBUTION_ENABLED default on after rollout `[evidence H]` (`backend/config.py`).
- **Documentation is co-shipped.** Every recent CHANGELOG entry explicitly lists which user-guide, developer-reference, or runbook files were updated alongside code `[evidence H]` (`CHANGELOG.md`).
- **Kanban and forensics are peers, not hierarchical.** The Feature Board and Session Inspector are equal first-class surfaces -- neither is a drill-down of the other `[inference H]` (`App.tsx`).

## Workflow / Delivery Model

Branching and delivery workflow is plan-driven: implementation plans live under `docs/project_plans/implementation_plans/` with phases, progress trackers under `.claude/progress/`, and the CHANGELOG is updated as each plan rolls out `[evidence H]` (`CHANGELOG.md`, `docs/project_plans/implementation_plans/`). The active branch feat/cli-mcp-enablement-codex follows this pattern with a phase-1 tracker committed.

Test execution uses vitest for the frontend and unittest/pytest for the backend; canonical invocations live in CLAUDE.md `[evidence H]` (`CLAUDE.md`, `package.json`, `vitest.config.ts`). No explicit CI config files (.github/workflows/) were surfaced in this pass -- whether GitHub Actions or another CI runs tests is `[open]`.

Dev ergonomics are prioritized: `npm run dev` boots both backend and frontend, `npm run dev:worker` runs the background worker standalone, and `scripts/setup.mjs` creates the Python venv `[evidence H]` (`package.json`). Hot reload is wired for both sides.

## Business / Product Assumptions

- **The user runs CCDash on their own machine against their own code.** The backend reads local paths, shells out to gh and git, and runs subprocesses from the execution runtime -- none of that is safe if the user does not fully trust the project directory `[inference H]` (`backend/services/execution_runtime.py`, `backend/config.py`).
- **One active project at a time.** projects.json plus sidebar switching implies a single foreground context per session `[inference M]` (`projects.json`).
- **Users trust their agent's session logs.** The ingestion pipeline accepts whatever the Claude Code / Codex parsers produce; there is no adversarial input model `[inference H]` (`backend/parsers/platforms/claude_code/parser.py`, `backend/parsers/platforms/codex/parser.py`).
- **Cost and token data is meaningful signal, not overhead.** Entire services and UI routes are dedicated to attribution, recommendations, and workflow effectiveness `[evidence H]` (`backend/services/session_usage_attribution.py`, `backend/services/workflow_effectiveness.py`).
- **External services are optional and gated.** Gemini insights, SAM telemetry export, SkillMeat publishing, and pricing sync are all opt-in `[evidence H]` (`backend/config.py`, `services/geminiService.ts`).
- **Upstream publishing requires human approval.** SkillMeat memory drafts are generated automatically but require operator approval in the Ops panel before publishing `[evidence H]` (`CHANGELOG.md`, `backend/services/integrations/skillmeat_memory_drafts.py`).

## Constraints and Tradeoffs

| Constraint | Where it appears | Tradeoff | Tag |
|---|---|---|---|
| Filesystem I/O latency bounds sync freshness | `backend/db/sync_engine.py`, `backend/db/file_watcher.py` | Accepts watcher complexity; near-real-time but not instant | `[evidence H]` |
| Single-tenant assumption pervades auth/runtime | `backend/runtime/profiles.py`, `backend/db/repositories/identity_access.py` (stub) | Multi-user requires a substantial uplift; PRD exists but no code | `[evidence H]` |
| Feature flags gate both UI and backend independently | `backend/config.py`, `services/agenticIntelligence.ts` | Enables safe rollout but requires dual-checks | `[evidence H]` |
| Router - Service - Repository layering strictly enforced | `CLAUDE.md`, `backend/routers/`, `backend/services/`, `backend/db/repositories/` | Prevents raw-SQL-in-router but adds indirection | `[evidence H]` |
| Large monolithic files in hot paths | `backend/db/sync_engine.py`, `backend/parsers/platforms/claude_code/parser.py`, `backend/routers/analytics.py`, `backend/models.py` | Concentrated refactor risk as they grow | `[inference H]` |
| OTEL or Prometheus fallback, not both | `backend/observability/otel.py`, `backend/config.py` | Simpler config but operators must choose | `[evidence M]` |

## Extensibility and Change Surface

- **New domain entities** are added by creating a parser (`backend/parsers/`), a repository (`backend/db/repositories/`), a service, and a router -- the layered convention makes this predictable `[inference H]`.
- **New session platforms** plug in via `backend/parsers/platforms/` and the routing layer in `backend/parsers/sessions.py` (Claude Code and Codex already exist in parallel) `[evidence H]`.
- **New background jobs** register through `backend/adapters/jobs/runtime.py` and a job module under `backend/adapters/jobs/` (pattern: `backend/adapters/jobs/telemetry_exporter.py`) `[evidence H]`.
- **New feature flags** are added to `backend/config.py` as CCDASH_* env-reading fields and surfaced to the frontend via `services/agenticIntelligence.ts` and context providers `[evidence H]`.
- **New UI routes** are added in `App.tsx` with a corresponding top-level component under components/ `[evidence H]`.
- **Agent-facing surfaces (CLI, MCP, VSCode)** are planned to share a transport-neutral query layer per ADR-003; Phase 1 foundation landed on the active branch `[evidence M]`.
- **Explicitly NOT extensible**: multi-tenant auth, remote execution (platform connectors are planned), and third-party plugin loading -- none of these have extension points today `[inference M]`.

## Technical Debt / Fragility Signals

- `backend/db/sync_engine.py` is the single biggest file in the repo and does hash tracking, entity routing, and change-detection for every domain -- any refactor here has global blast radius `[inference H]`.
- `backend/parsers/platforms/claude_code/parser.py` is the second largest file and is the bridge between external log format and the canonical session store -- tightly coupled to Claude Code's evolving output `[inference H]`.
- `backend/routers/analytics.py` is oversized and covers both standard metrics and session-intelligence aggregations; splitting into analytics-metrics vs. session-intelligence routers would likely improve clarity `[inference M]`.
- `backend/models.py` holds Pydantic models across many domains; cross-domain changes can cascade `[inference M]`.
- `backend/db/repositories/identity_access.py` exists as a stub; relying on it for anything real today is unsafe `[inference H]`.
- Three experimental flags default off (CCDASH_TEST_VISUALIZER_ENABLED, CCDASH_INTEGRITY_SIGNALS_ENABLED, CCDASH_LIVE_TEST_UPDATES_ENABLED); the UI routes exist but downstream behavior when on is only partially validated by tests `[evidence M]` (`backend/config.py`).
- No CI config surfaced in this pass -- cannot confirm from the repo alone whether every push is test-gated `[open]`.
- Doc/code coupling is tight right now but depends on human discipline of updating CHANGELOG and guides with every rollout; no automation enforces it `[inference M]`.

## What Future Contributors Must Understand First

1. **The filesystem is the truth.** Any change you make to code without understanding how `backend/db/sync_engine.py` reads and writes the cache will desync the UI.
2. **Router - Service - Repository is non-negotiable.** Routers must not call repositories or raw SQL; services must not call routers.
3. **Runtime profiles gate capabilities.** Before writing a feature, check whether it runs under local, api, worker, or test and whether the profile's capabilities (watch, sync, jobs, auth, integrations) are on.
4. **Storage profile matters.** local SQLite is the default; enterprise Postgres opts into a different capability matrix -- check `backend/runtime/storage_contract.py`.
5. **Canonical session transcripts live in session_messages, not the raw log.** Anything reading sessions must go through the projection/contract services.
6. **Entity linking is universal.** Use `backend/db/repositories/entity_graph.py`, not per-feature foreign keys, for cross-entity references.
7. **The frontend useData() is a compatibility facade.** New features should consume the specific shell contexts (AppSessionContext, AppEntityDataContext, AppRuntimeContext) directly.
8. **Feature flags gate both UI and backend.** Default-off flags must be safe to enable; default-on flags are production behavior.
9. **External integrations are opt-in** and must degrade cleanly when keys/endpoints are absent.
10. **Plan before you code.** Expect to write or update a plan under `docs/project_plans/` and a progress tracker under `.claude/progress/` before merging significant work.

## Strategic Implications for Further Research

Cheap changes: **new analytics endpoints**, **new session-intelligence facts**, **new UI routes**, and **new feature flags** all ride the existing layering and can be added without reshaping the architecture -- this is where most agentic-intelligence work has landed `[inference H]`.

Expensive changes: **anything multi-tenant**, anything that requires a new storage engine, anything that bypasses `backend/db/sync_engine.py`, and anything that removes the local-first default. These touch the profile matrix, the layering convention, and the filesystem-as-truth assumption simultaneously `[inference H]`.

Dangerous changes: **edits to `backend/db/sync_engine.py` or `backend/parsers/platforms/claude_code/parser.py`** without strong test coverage. These are the highest-footprint files, and CCDash's entire value proposition rests on them producing correct entity graphs. The fact that TODO debt is near-zero suggests the team fixes rather than papers over -- a refactor here should follow that same pattern `[inference H]`.

## Open Questions / Ambiguities

1. Where does CI actually run, and what gates PR merges? .github/workflows/ not surfaced in this pass.
2. Is there a desktop shell (Electron/Tauri) packaging, or is CCDash purely localhost web?
3. Which embedding provider backs `backend/db/repositories/session_embeddings.py`?
4. Is the VSCode extension from ADR-001 in a sibling repo or still paper-only?
5. What is the observed rebuild cost of a full sync from scratch, and does it bound project size?
